title: Removal from List of NAIFs by AllCap Multi-Fund Platform SICAV plc
text:
LEI Code: 5299007TB6615KIB9859 
 This notice is to make the general public aware that Audentia Capital Management Ltd (“the AIFM”) requested the Malta Financial Services Authority (“MFSA”) to accept the removal of Audentia World FX Fund (“the Sub-Fund”) from the MFSA’s list of Notified AIFs (“NAIFs”). This removal is entirely voluntary and does not arise as a result of any regulatory action taken by the MFSA. Accordingly, with effect from 16 April 2026 , the Sub-Fund ceased to form part of the MFSA’s list of NAIFs.

title: Regulatory Action against Recognised Incorporated Cell Company – Ref: 2026-06
text:
The Malta Financial Services Authority (“MFSA”) decided to proceed with imposing an administrative penalty of five hundred Euro (€500) on the Recognised Incorporated Cell Company. 
 BREACHES 
 The MFSA determined that the Recognised Incorporated Cell Company has acted in breach of SLC 6.4(i) of Part BIII of the Investment Services Rules for Recognised Persons as it failed to submit the Letter from the Board of Directors for the financial year ended 31 December 2022 within the regulatory deadline. 
 REGULATORY ACTION 
 On 9 April 2026, the MFSA decided to impose an administrative penalty of five hundred Euro (€500) on the Recognised Incorporated Cell Company. 
 PUBLICATION 
 This notice is being published in terms of Article 16(8) of the Malta Financial Services Authority Act and the MFSA’s Publication Policy.

title: Removal from the List of Notified AIFs by 24 Capital Management SICAV p.l.c.
text: This notice is to make the general public aware that 24 Capital Management Limited (the “AIFM”) requested the Malta Financial Services Authority (“MFSA”) to accept the removal of 24 Global Multi Asset Fund (the “Sub-Fund”) from the MFSA’s List of Notified AIFs (“NAIFs”). This removal is entirely voluntary and does not arise as a result of any regulatory action taken by the MFSA. Accordingly, with effect from 15 April 2026 , the Sub-Fund ceased to form part of the MFSA’s List of NAIFs.

title: Professional Investor Fund – Ref: 2026-15
text:
The Malta Financial Services Authority (“MFSA” or the “Authority”) has been looking into certain matters related to a Professional Investor Fund with respect to the late submission of regulatory information to the Authority. The Authority has considered the goodwill demonstrated by the Professional Investor Fund and agreed to settle all pending matters. 
 Business Activity : Professional Investor Fund 
 FINDINGS: 
 The MFSA found failings in respect to SLC 4.1 of Appendix 1 to Part B of the Rules for PIFs with respect to the late submission of the Auditor’s Confirmation for financial year ended 31 December 2021. 
 MEASURES 
 On 13 April 2026, the MFSA agreed to settle pending matters with the Professional Investor Fund after demonstration of goodwill by the Professional Investor Fund. By means of a settlement agreement entered into between the Professional Investor Fund and the MFSA, the latter imposed an administrative penalty of four hundred and fifty-five Euro (€455) on the Professional Investor Fund. 
 PUBLICATION 
 This notice is being published in terms of the Authority’s Settlement Policy .

title: Professional Investor Fund – Ref: 2026-14
text:
The Malta Financial Services Authority (“MFSA” or the “Authority”) has been looking into certain matters related to a Professional Investor Fund with respect to the late submission of regulatory information to the Authority. The Authority has considered the goodwill demonstrated by the Professional Investor Fund and agreed to settle all pending matters. 
 Business Activity : Professional Investor Fund 
 FINDINGS: 
 The MFSA found failings in respect to SLC 1.62 Part BII of the Investment Services Rules for PIFs with respect to the non-submission of the Annual Fund Return, Fund Return Representation Sheet, and Auditor’s Report on the Annual Fund Return for financial year ended 31 March 2022. 
 MEASURES 
 On 10 April 2026, the MFSA agreed to settle pending matters with the Professional Investor Fund after demonstration of goodwill by the Professional Investor Fund. By means of a settlement agreement entered into between the Professional Investor Fund and the MFSA, the latter imposed an administrative penalty of nine hundred Euro (€900) on the Professional Investor Fund. 
 PUBLICATION 
 This notice is being published in terms of the Authority’s Settlement Policy .

title: Collective Investment Scheme – Ref: 2026-05
text:
The Malta Financial Services Authority (“MFSA”) decided to proceed with imposing an administrative penalty of five hundred Euro (€500) on the Retail UCITS Scheme. 
 BREACHES 
 The MFSA determined that the Retail UCITS Scheme has acted in breach of SLC 12.8 of Part BII of the Investment Services Rules for Retail Schemes as it failed to submit the Financial Derivatives Position Report for the financial year ended 31 December 2022 within the regulatory deadline. 
 REGULATORY ACTION 
 On 8 April 2026, the MFSA decided to impose an administrative penalty of five hundred Euro (€500) on the Retail UCITS Scheme. 
 PUBLICATION 
 This notice is being published in terms of Article 16(8) of the Malta Financial Services Authority Act and the MFSA’s Publication Policy.

title: Collective Investment Scheme – Ref: 2026-04
text:
The Malta Financial Services Authority (“MFSA”) decided to proceed with imposing an administrative penalty of one thousand and one hundred Euro (€1,100) on the Retail UCITS Scheme. 
 BREACHES 
 The MFSA determined that the Retail UCITS Scheme has acted in breach of SLC 1.3(i) of Appendix II to Part B of the Investment Services Rules for Retail Schemes and SLC 12.8 of Part BII of the Investment Services Rules for Retail Schemes as it failed to submit the Director’s Confirmation (related to the annual reporting) and the Self-Assessment Certificate for the financial year ended 31 December 2022 within the regulatory deadline. 
 REGULATORY ACTION 
 On 8 April 2026, the MFSA decided to impose an administrative penalty of one thousand and one hundred Euro (€1,100) on the Retail UCITS Scheme. 
 PUBLICATION 
 This notice is being published in terms of Article 16(8) of the Malta Financial Services Authority Act and the MFSA’s Publication Policy.

title: Surrender of Licence by Ledbury SICAV plc
text:
LEI Code: 213800PA4X57E91HJ960 
 This notice is to make the general public aware that as a result of a recent merger between the Hanson Sustainable Income Fund and another scheme’s sub-fund, Ledbury SICAV plc (“the Scheme”) requested the Malta Financial Services Authority (“MFSA”) to accept the surrender of the collective investment scheme licence granted to the Scheme. This surrender is entirely voluntary and does not arise as a result of any regulatory action taken by the MFSA. Accordingly, with effect from 13 April 2026, the Scheme ceased to be licensed by the MFSA.

title: Surrender of Licence by J&T SICAV plc
text: This notice is to make the general public aware that J&T SICAV plc (the ”Scheme”), with LEI code: 315700O70NU25CFZTV76, requested the Malta Financial Services Authority (“MFSA”) to accept the surrender of the collective investment scheme licence granted to the Scheme and its Sub-Funds, namely, J&T Dividend Fund, with LEI code: 315700L36SU5E0AFG451 and J&T Rentier Fund, with LEI code: 315700255SLD81SKMZ37 . This surrender is entirely voluntary and does not arise as a result of any regulatory action taken by the MFSA. Accordingly, with effect from 08 April 2026, the Scheme and its Sub-Funds ceased to be licensed by the MFSA.
